capsules.name: Fix Subagent Message Delivery in Telegram Context
capsules.trigger: Subagent spawned from cron/heartbeat fails to deliver messages to Telegram
steps: Identify: grep gateway log for 'Unknown target' or 'requires target <chatId>'
Root cause: subagent lacks implicit chat context — cron runs have no originating chatId
Fix: use sessions_spawn (auto-announces to requester) instead of message tool with target
If direct messaging needed: use sessions_send to relay through parent session
Never use target='main' — Telegram requires numeric chatId

capsules.name: Diagnose Chronic Subagent Announce Retry Loop
capsules.trigger: Same runId appears in 'Subagent completion direct announce failed' 5+ times across 3+ hours
steps: Extract runId: grep 'Subagent completion direct announce failed' gateway log | extract run UUID
Count occurrences and time span to confirm chronic (not transient burst)
Root cause: gateway retry loop — run completed but announce target unreachable, retries indefinitely
This is a gateway-level issue, not fixable from agent side
Action: log as known pattern, do not restart gateway (won't help), monitor for resolution on next gateway update
capsules.origin: PCEC #13 — 2026-02-22, run bf97824f retried 14 times over 12 hours

capsules.name: Diagnose and Recover from LLM Provider Cascade Failure
capsules.trigger: Multiple lanes fail simultaneously with timeout + 502; 'All models failed' in gateway log
steps: Detect: grep gateway log for 'All models failed' or count simultaneous 'LLM request timed out' across lanes
Verify cascade: check if failures span main + cron + session lanes within a 15-min window
Root cause: all models route through same upstream (e.g., deeprouter → Anthropic); single point of failure
Immediate: no action needed — system auto-recovers when upstream returns; expect queued cron batch-fire
Post-recovery: check for duplicate cron deliveries from batch-fire (multiple PCEC triggers arriving together)
Prevention: configure at least one fallback model on a different upstream provider for critical cron jobs (e.g., MiniMax M2.5 as cron fallback)
capsules.origin: PCEC #14 — 2026-02-22, 12-min total outage caused 3 PCEC triggers to batch

capsules.name: Multi-Agent Report Routing: subagent → main → user
capsules.trigger: Subagent (e.g. dev-mgr) sends progress report via sessions_send to main session
steps: Receive sessions_send from subagent (e.g., dev-mgr reports 'issue #N complete')
Evaluate: worth notifying user? (task complete/failed, error, important progress → YES; routine status, no change → NO)
If NO → respond with NO_REPLY, do nothing else
If YES → use message tool to send notification to Telegram (this is the ONLY reliable path to user from inter-session context)
After sending via message tool → respond with NO_REPLY to avoid duplicate output
NEVER reply conversationally to the subagent — it's an AI, waste of tokens
NEVER use [[reply_to_current]] — in inter-session context this routes back to the sending session, not to Telegram
capsules.origin: PCEC #17 — 2026-02-23, extracted from 2026-02-22 memory lesson about dev-mgr notification failure

capsules.name: Fix Cron Tool Parameter Format Errors
capsules.trigger: cron.update or cron.add fails with 'unexpected property' or 'must have required property text'
steps: STOP: do not re-read jobs.json and try again with the same format
The internal jobs.json uses 'message' but the tool API uses 'text' for the prompt content
For cron.update: use 'jobId' (not 'id') as the job identifier
For payload: use { text: '...' } — not { message: '...', model: '...', agentId: '...' }
Model, agentId, timeoutSeconds, thinking may be separate tool params, not nested in payload
If format is still unclear: run 'openclaw cron --help' via exec to discover correct CLI params
Alternative: edit ~/.openclaw/cron/jobs.json directly with the write tool, then restart gateway to apply changes
capsules.origin: PCEC #21 — 2026-02-23, main session failed 4x with wrong cron params before resorting to delete+recreate

capsules.name: Escalate Recurring Mistakes Beyond Gene Documentation
capsules.trigger: Same gene triggers 3+ times across separate PCEC cycles despite documentation
steps: Identify: review events.jsonl for repeated gene triggers on the same error pattern
Root cause: gene documents the fix but agent keeps making the same mistake — documentation alone is insufficient
Escalate level 1: add a concrete note to TOOLS.md with the correct value/mapping (agent reads TOOLS.md every session)
Escalate level 2: if still recurring, check if the tool itself can enforce the constraint (e.g., parameter validation)
Escalate level 3: if tool-level fix is impossible, add to AGENTS.md as a mandatory check rule
Example: web_search search_lang='zh' keeps failing → add 'Chinese search: use zh-hans not zh' to TOOLS.md
Track escalation in the gene's origin field: append 'escalated to TOOLS.md YYYY-MM-DD'
capsules.origin: PCEC #67 — 2026-03-03, stagnation breaker: search_lang=zh recurred 3x despite gene existing since Feb 26
